channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Uruguay's new leftist president takes office, facing a financial balancing act
item.description: MONTEVIDEO, Uruguay (AP) — Yamandú Orsi, a telegenic left-leaning former mayor and history teacher, took office as Uruguay's new president on Saturday, at the helm of a government that has pledged to strengthen the social safety net while reversing years of economic stagnation.
item.dc:creator: MATILDE CAMPODONICO and ISABEL DEBRE, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: In address to congress, Argentine President Milei promises IMF deal and lauds economic wins
item.description: Argentina’s libertarian president Javier Milei has signaled that a new and much-anticipated deal with the International Monetary Fund is imminent.
item.dc:creator: Isabel Debre, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Israel agrees to Trump envoy’s temporary Gaza ceasefire extension proposal as first phase expires: reports
item.description: As the first phase of the temporary ceasefire in Gaza ends, the Israeli government said Saturday night it had agreed to a proposal by Trump envoy Steve Witkoff to extend it.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Israel supports a US proposal to extend Gaza ceasefire's first phase. But Hamas wants Phase 2
item.description: CAIRO (AP) — Israel’s government said early Sunday it supports a proposal to extend the first phase of the ceasefire in Gaza through Ramadan and Passover, though Hamas has insisted on negotiating the truce’s second phase instead.
item.dc:creator: SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Israel supports a US proposal to extend Gaza ceasefire's first phase. But Hamas wants Phase 2
item.description: CAIRO (AP) — Israel’s government said early Sunday it supports a proposal to extend the first phase of the ceasefire in Gaza through Ramadan and Passover, though Hamas has insisted on negotiating the truce’s second phase instead.
item.dc:creator: SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, The Hill

item.title: Zelenskyy meets with British Prime Minister Keir Starmer in London following Trump Oval Office clash
item.description: A day after his White House clash with President Trump, Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy was greeted in London by Prime Minister Keir Starmer.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Pope Francis stable a day after respiratory crisis, spent 'long periods' off noninvasive ventilator
item.description: ROME (AP) — Pope Francis remained in stable condition Saturday a day after a respiratory crisis during his battle with double pneumonia, the Vatican said. He spent long periods of the day off the noninvasive mechanical ventilation he initially needed in a sign that his lung function was improving.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD and GIOVANNA DELL'ORTO, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Israel's military is told to prepare to defend a Druze community outside Syria's capital
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — Israel’s defense ministry on Saturday said the military has been instructed to prepare to defend a Druze settlement in the suburbs of Damascus, asserting that the minority it has vowed to protect was “under attack” by Syrian forces.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Zelenskyy embraced by British prime minister a day after White House blowout
item.description: British Prime Minister Keir Starmer has told Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy he has the nation’s unwavering support a day after the blowout at the White House with President Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: Brian Melley, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Vatican gives health update as Pope enters third week in hospital
item.description: Pope Francis' condition is stable, and he is alternating between mechanical ventilation and high-flow oxygen therapy, the Vatican said on Saturday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Turkey's Kurds wary of path to peace after PKK declares ceasefire
item.description: DIYARBAKIR, Turkey (AP) — A momentous ceasefire declaration on Saturday stirred a mix of emotions in southeast Turkey and northern Iraq, where people bore the brunt of the 40-year conflict between Kurdish militants and the Turkish state.
item.dc:creator: MUCAHIT CEYLAN and ADNAN AHMAD, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Ukrainians won't stop fighting, even if US pulls support: Veteran
item.description: Carl Larson of Seattle fought on Ukraine’s front lines for six months, an experience he describes as “horrific.
item.dc:creator: Michael Ramsey, The Hill

item.title: Ukrainians rally around Zelenskyy as defender of national interest after Oval Office blowout
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Soon after Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy left the White House on Friday after an astonishing Oval Office blowout with President Donald Trump, Ukrainians rallied around Zelenskyy as a defender of his country's interests.
item.dc:creator: JUSTIN SPIKE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Sean Penn: Zelensky-Trump fight shouldn't eclipse Russia's crimes
item.description: “Remember where we really stand and what this is really about,” Sean Penn implored on Friday's "CUOMO.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, The Hill

item.title: Kurdish PKK militants declare a ceasefire in 40-year insurgency in Turkey
item.description: Kurdish militants who have waged a 40-year insurgency in Turkey have declared a ceasefire, just two days after their imprisoned leader called for the group to disarm.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Ukrainians won't stop fighting, even if US pulls support: Veteran
item.description: Carl Larson of Seattle fought on Ukraine’s front lines for six months, an experience he describes as “horrific.
item.dc:creator: Michael Ramsey, NewsNation

item.title: Pope suffers breathing crisis amid pneumonia battle, Vatican says
item.description: Following the episode, the pope began non-invasive ventilation and was responding well, according to the Vatican.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Philippines shark attack: 2 tourists killed at popular diving spot
item.description: Two tourists reportedly were killed by sharks in the Philippines near Verde Island after getting swept away by a current while diving at a popular location
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: UN talks end in Rome with nations backing $200 billion a year plan to protect nature
item.description: BOGOTA, Colombia (AP) — Global negotiators concluded an extended session of the United Nations biodiversity conference, COP16, with key commitments on funds needed and the institutions through which the funds will be channeled to protect the world's biodiversity.
item.dc:creator: STEVEN GRATTAN and SIBI ARASU, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Morocco urges people to not buy sheep for Eid al-Adha celebrations
item.description: OULAD SLAMA, Morocco (AP) — Sheep come running when Larbi El Ghazouani pours alfalfa and straw into their troughs twice a day.
item.dc:creator: SAM METZ and AKRAM OUBACHIR, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Panama and Costa Rica turning into a 'black hole' for migrants and deportees from US, observers warn
item.description: MIRAMAR, Panama (AP) — Officials in Costa Rica and Panama are confiscating migrants’ passports and cellphones, denying them access to legal services and moving them between remote outposts as they wrestle with the logistics of a suddenly reversed migration flow.
item.dc:creator: MEGAN JANETSKY, MATÍAS DELACROIX and JOSHUA GOODMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Pope Francis continues to improve but his prognosis remains guarded: Vatican
item.description: Pope Francis continued his slow recovery from double pneumonia on Thursday, beating back speculation of an imminent death, resignation or conclave and signalling that he was still very much in charge albeit in a weakened state.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Pope Francis' condition continues to improve as he receives oxygen therapy: Vatican
item.description: Pope Francis' condition is improving as he undergoes high-flow oxygen therapy, the Vatican said in a statement. The 88-year-old pontiff has been hospitalized for nearly two weeks.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Female American tourist, 37, goes missing during jet ski ride in Thailand: report
item.description: A 37-year-old American tourist reportedly vanished Thursday in Thailand while jet skiing along Patong Beach near Phuket, prompting a search, a report says.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Hamas hands over bodies of 4 hostages to Israel as dozens of Palestinians leave Israeli prison
item.description: KHAN YOUNIS, Gaza Strip (AP) — Hamas handed over the bodies of four hostages to the Red Cross early Thursday in exchange for Israel's release of hundreds of Palestinian prisoners, days before the first phase of the ceasefire in the Gaza Strip was to end.
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA, SAMY MAGDY and JOSEF FEDERMAN, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Man forced to sit next to corpse after woman died midway through flight
item.description: Passenger Mitchell Ring said the flight crew did everything they could to revive the woman but they were unsuccessful.
item.dc:creator: Emily Smith, NewsNation

item.title: Pope Francis shows slight improvement and resumes some work, while still critical, Vatican says
item.description: ROME (AP) — Pope Francis remained in critical condition Monday but showed slight improvement in laboratory tests and resumed some work, the Vatican said, including calling a parish in Gaza City that he has kept in touch with since the war there began.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD and TRISHA THOMAS, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Western leaders visit Kyiv and pledge military support against Russia on the war's 3rd anniversary
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Ukraine on Monday marked the bleakest anniversary yet of its war against the Russia invasion, with the country's forces under severe pressure on the battlefield and U.S. President Donald Trump's administration apparently embracing the Kremlin in a reversal of American policy.
item.dc:creator: JUSTIN SPIKE, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Conservative opposition wins German election and the far right is 2nd with strongest postwar result
item.description: BERLIN (AP) — The opposition conservatives led by Friedrich Merz won a lackluster victory in Germany's election Sunday and Alternative for Germany doubled its support in the strongest showing for a far-right party since World War II, projections showed.
item.dc:creator: GEIR MOULSON, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Ukraine's Zelenskyy says he'd be ready to give up presidency if it brought peace, NATO membership
item.description: “If to achieve peace, you really need me to give up my post, I’m ready,” Zelenskyy said in response to a journalist's question on whether he’d trade his office for peace. “I can trade it for NATO.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Surviving Hamas: The story of Keith Siegel, an Israeli American hostage
item.description: An Israeli-American released from Hamas captivity weeks ago is doing 'OK,' his wife tells NewsNation's "Morning in America with Hena Doba.
item.dc:creator: Ashley N. Soriano, NewsNation

item.title: Afghan women’s radio station will resume broadcasts after Taliban lift suspension
item.description: Radio Begum launched on International Women’s Day in March 2021, five months before the Taliban seized power.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, NewsNation
